ctxMarket: Solana is drawing attention for surpassing Ethereum in real-world asset (RWA) lending and attracting increased institutional inflows in Q1. Broader financial use cases and security concerns are in focus, alongside ongoing product launches in the DeFi space.
ctxTalking:
• Solana overtaking Ethereum in RWA lending and seeing more institutional participation
• A fake Ledger app on Apple’s App Store linked to multi-chain crypto theft, including Solana assets
• Speculation around X’s teased crypto product and potential Solana connections
ctxMatters: Solana’s growth in RWA lending signals expanding adoption beyond trading, potentially increasing its relevance for institutional players. Security incidents like the fake Ledger app highlight ongoing risks for asset holders. New DeFi products and speculation about major platform integrations could influence Solana’s ecosystem development.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Solana’s lead in RWA lending and rising institutional flows point to tangible ecosystem shifts.
Noise: Speculation around X’s crypto product remains unconfirmed and lacks concrete details.
ctxGlossary:
RWA lending — Lending involving real-world assets, such as property or loans, on blockchain platforms
DeFi — Decentralized finance; financial services using blockchain technology without intermediaries

readMore.title: Solana flips Ethereum in RWA lending as institutional flows rise in Q1
Figure and Hastra widen DeFi credit offering with auto loan launch
Fake Ledger app on Apple App Store linked to $9.5M crypto theft across Bitcoin, Tron, Solana
